title: The Main Function
description: Learn about the entry point of every Kotlin program.

slides.title: Where does it begin? 🏁
slides.content:
## The Start Line

When you run a Kotlin program, the computer needs to know *where* to start reading your code. 

It looks for a very specific block of code known as the **`main` function**.

slides.title: The Main Structure 🏗️
slides.content: This is what the main function looks like. Any code you place inside the curly braces `{ ... }` will be executed when the program runs.

quizData.question: What is the name of the function where Kotlin programs start executing?
quizData.explanation: Every Kotlin program execution begins at the `main()` function.

slides.title: Let's break it down 🧩
slides.content:
## Syntax Breakdown

- `fun`: This keyword is short for **function**. It tells Kotlin we are creating a reusable block of code.
- `main`: This is the **name** of the function. It must be named exactly "main".
- `()`: These parentheses can hold extra information (arguments), but are often left empty.
- `{ }`: These curly braces hold the actual instructions for the computer to follow.

quizData.question: Which keyword is used to declare a function in Kotlin?
quizData.explanation: Kotlin uses the neat and concise `fun` keyword to declare functions.

slides.title: Putting it together ⚙️
slides.content: To print "Hello, World!", we must place the `println()` statement inside our `main` function.
